level_1_reorder.detail: all 3 orderings failed — this is NOT a prompt-ordering problem

level_1_5_context_enrichment.detail: proven lemma registry with exact names fixes the hallucination problem

level_2_decomposition.detail: decomposed into 4 sub-lemmas (~5-30 LOC each) + 15 LOC assembly

main_body_assembly.first_attempt: FAIL (postcondition not satisfied — SMT couldn't close final arithmetic)
main_body_assembly.fix_description: added explicit intermediate assertions for arithmetic steps

level_2_decomposition.cost: 1 decomposition plan + 4 sub-lemma generations + 1 main body + 2 fix cycles = 8 LLM calls

F_mechanical.recommended: Level 1.5 is cheaper (1 call vs 8) and produces more concise proof (91 vs ~58 LOC)

level_1_reorder.detail: V2 produces correct proof at 34 LOC. Different prompt ordering avoids the type error.

level_0_standard_pipeline.description: Single prompt variant + 5 compile-fix cycles + 2 strategy retries

level_1_prompt_reorder.description: Retry with different prompt ordering (up to 2 reorderings)

level_1_5_context_enrichment.description: Add proven lemma registry with exact signatures
level_1_5_context_enrichment.note: This should be Level 1, not 1.5 — it's cheaper than reordering and more effective

level_2_decomposition.description: Break into sub-lemmas, generate each independently
level_2_decomposition.incremental_recovery: recovers >50 LOC proofs that fail monolithic generation
level_2_decomposition.cost: ~8 LLM calls (plan + N sub-lemmas + assembly + fixes)

level_5_admit.description: Leave admit(), flag for human review

recommended_cascade: Level 1: Add proven lemma registry (cheapest, highest-value supplement — 18b finding)
Level 2: Prompt reorder (try V2 sibling-first, then V3 strategy-first)
Level 3: Decompose into sub-lemmas (for >50 LOC or persistent failures)
Level 4: Skip and revisit (after other functions provide new siblings)

key_findings.finding_1: Context enrichment (proven lemma registry) should be BEFORE prompt reorder in the cascade — it's cheaper (1 call vs 2) and recovers the hardest failures (name hallucination).
key_findings.finding_2: Decomposition WORKS for the 50 LOC ceiling — all 4 sub-lemmas passed first-shot, only the assembly needed 2 fix cycles. Total cost: 8 LLM calls.
key_findings.finding_3: The decomposition agent produced a clean 4-step plan without seeing the GT. The plan was structurally similar to the GT proof's 5-step approach — both identified the same key intermediate facts.
key_findings.finding_4: Assembly is the hardest part of decomposition — the SMT solver needs explicit arithmetic stepping stones to connect sub-lemma results to the final postcondition.
